title: 2-Hydroxy-5-Chloro Acetophenone Oxime as a Gravimetric  Reagent for Oxo-Vanadium(V)
author: NEPAL SINGH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Hindu College, Moradabad. 

  Manuscript received 17 January 1978, revised 19,September 1978, accepted 6 October 1978.  

 Oxo-vanadium(V) has been precipitated quantitatively from the solution in the  pH  range 3.5-6.5 with 2-hydroxy-5-chloro acetophenone oxime and determined by weighing the complex VO(OH) (C 8 H 7 CINO 2 ) 2  after drying at 110-120°. The reagent has been used in the separation and gravimetric determination of vanadium(V) in its binary mixture with copper. The interference due to other ions has been avoided either using suitable masking agent or by working at controlled  p H. I. R. spectral studies have also been carried out to assign a tentative structure to the complex.
